summary: Updated the Nix-packaged m2c dependency by changing the pinned matt-kempster/m2c GitHub revision and corresponding fixed-output sha256 hash in .nix/m2c.nix. This keeps the Nix build using a newer m2c snapshot while preserving reproducible fetchFromGitHub pinning.
what_changed: In .nix/m2c.nix, the fetchFromGitHub source for owner "matt-kempster" repo "m2c" was bumped from rev "4caccf86a027bf52b9132a8ae9331cb3210305a8" to "f201e8861ba0143676f3078d7354d2f7255555b3".
The Nix fixed-output hash was updated from "sha256-dutmRYEP1XQwnbrG3d/VZdldl4x+pWxq84aj1xTKApI=" to "sha256-WhXIeeiRLDeVSgbIuCAlJVTLHtFbJGsM+ACmhsAPUvk=".
No source decompilation files, headers, symbols, or assembly matching code were changed in the provided diff.
smart_moves: Kept the m2c dependency pinned to an exact Git commit rather than a floating branch, preserving reproducibility for Nix users.
Updated the corresponding Nix sha256 hash in the same patch as the revision bump, which is required for fetchFromGitHub fixed-output derivations to evaluate successfully.
Limited the PR to the minimal build-tooling change needed for the Nix build: two lines changed in a single file.
decomp_lessons: Tooling updates for decomp workflows should remain reproducibly pinned, especially when the tool affects generated C or matching experiments.
When bumping a Nix dependency fetched from GitHub, always update both rev and hash together; changing only rev will break the fixed-output derivation.
Small, isolated dependency-pin PRs are easier to review and revert than mixing tooling bumps with decompilation source changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were present in the PR evidence. The change only updates the pinned m2c tooling used by the build environment.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context. The dump indicates one issue comment but no excerpt/body was available, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify .nix/m2c.nix to see whether this m2c revision was superseded or caused build issues.
Search for PRs using m2c-generated code after this bump to determine whether the new m2c snapshot affected decompilation workflow.
Inspect the unavailable issue comment for PR 1975 if comment-body data becomes available.
